posts.text: Hi Rajesh. Thanks for sharing this. As I had gone through this transcript and I did some shallow research yet, my understanding is S.H Kelker is also spending 2-3% on R&D if I am not mistaken. Could you provide some more color on R&D spend of some other competitors if they have any. Sacheerome seems like better option because of their integration in customer product for long time if they can execute and keep spending more on R&D.

posts.author: sanketkulkarni1987
posts.community: Sacheerome Ltd (NSE - SME: SACHEEROME) A Niche Play on India’s Fragrance & Flavours Sector
posts.text: I have been trying to understand this company for the past few weeks. Some questions that I am not able to wrap my head around Company is expanding capacity from 7.6 lakh kg to 27.6 lakh kg. Total capex is around 184 cr. Company has spent 47 cr from internal accruals and will also use IPO proceeds. Even after that they will fall short of roughly 60 cr for which they will use debt. Now my question is if the capacity is going to be more long term in nature then why spend so much money now ? Wouldnt it have made more sense to add roughly same as current capacity for next 3-4 years and then once i…

posts.author: sameernics
posts.community: Sacheerome Ltd (NSE - SME: SACHEEROME) A Niche Play on India’s Fragrance & Flavours Sector
posts.text: Just wondering… with West Asia war, isn’t entire FMCG consumption exposed as the oil prices elevated for a prolonged period of time? The full impact of inflation I guess will play out over the course of the year and that is when one will start to see big cutbacks on consumption and hence the demand would slow down for Sacheerome and a possible impact to their expansion plans too ?

posts.author: rk1771
posts.community: Sacheerome Ltd (NSE - SME: SACHEEROME) A Niche Play on India’s Fragrance & Flavours Sector
posts.text: Sacheerome is not a commodity player, and hence ramp up capacity will not increase the topline like commodity steel or cement player. The company work on brief to product model. Sacheerome’s R&D team works to win these specific briefs. Once won, the formulation is locked into the client’s product line, creating annuity-like recurring revenue. Winning and migrating these high-volume briefs takes time. Yes, ramp up capacity does give a headroom to grow. The company has increased it’s topline from 70 crores to 152 crores in last 3 year, a CAGR close to 25 percent. As management guided, this looks…

posts.text: This company came to my radar last week. Thanks for starting the thread. I understand promoters want to be conservative, but even if we consider 50% utilisation (new plant) in FY28 still revenue would be around 370 cr, while promoters guided for 250cr.
